songContext.summary: A swaggering, celebratory track that mixes braggadocio about wealth, travel and flashy possessions with flirtatious devotion and brief nods to humble roots. The singer boasts of luxury shopping, international movement and car details while insisting he’s a devoted fan of one woman; recurring lines also stress that change or payoff ‘‘will take time.’’
themes: Romantic loyalty / fan of one woman
Patience and time needed for change
songContext.tone: Confident, playful and boastful with affectionate moments and a touch of nostalgia
notablePhrases: Utte nu main chhe chhe inch chakkiyan — "On which I have placed six-inch wheels"
Fan ikko naar da main — "I am a loyal admirer of only one woman"
Time laggu mitne nu — "It will take much time"
Karaan shopping'an Milan Afghan 'ali kha ke — "I shop in Milan, enjoying Afghan treats"
Nitt UAE ton UK nu rotation'an rekaane — (repeated idea of frequent UAE–UK rotations/travel)
Sanu kinna kuj de giyan ni mitti diyan vattan — "These dirt paths have given us so much"
Dil todte main chhe chhe saak modte — "I broke six hearts, turned six corners"
Tera sau ton paar para hogyi dhilli kuj khaa ke — "Your level/temperature went above 100 as you ate something from Delhi"
Das vaari vaarda main — "More than ten times"
Ikki vaari jor laa lai chhatti'an — "Even if you try 21 times with all your strength"

lines.original: Show Mxrci on it! he-he-he!
lines.literal: Show Mxrci on it! he-he-he!
lines.natural: Show Merc on it! he-he-he!
lines.slangAware: Put the Merc on it! heh-heh!
lines.chosen: Show Merc on it! he-he-he!
lines.translated: Show Merc on it! he-he-he!
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Show Mxrci on it! he-he-he!' — 'Mxrci' is a stylized spelling of 'Merc' (Mercedes). Kept the show-off exclamation literal.
lines.selectorReason: Chose the literal/natural rendering to preserve the stylized brand reference and onomatopoeic laugh; balances accuracy and lyrical feel.

lines.original: (Haan)
lines.literal: (Haan)
lines.natural: (Yes)
lines.slangAware: (Yeah)
lines.chosen: (Yes)
lines.translated: (Yes)
lines.note: Transliteration: '(Haan)'. Simple affirmation; 'Yes' is the neutral English equivalent.
lines.selectorReason: Direct, unambiguous translation of the filler interjection.

lines.original: Do do ghode Magna ni
lines.literal: Do do ghode Magna ni
lines.natural: I ask for two horses
lines.slangAware: I'm asking for two horses
lines.chosen: I ask for two horses
lines.translated: I ask for two horses
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Do do ghode Magna ni'. Could be literal or a metaphor for asking for powerful rides; kept conservative literal rendering.
lines.selectorReason: Retained literal meaning to avoid imposing an interpretation (e.g., 'rides' vs 'horses').

lines.original: Char G-Wagon'an ni
lines.literal: Char G-Wagon'an ni
lines.natural: Four G‑Wagons
lines.slangAware: Four G‑Wagons, yeah
lines.chosen: Four G‑Wagons
lines.translated: Four G‑Wagons
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Char G-Wagon'an ni'. G‑Wagon = Mercedes G‑Class SUVs; direct numeric phrase.
lines.selectorReason: Clear reference to vehicles; literal rendering is accurate and natural.

lines.original: Utte nu main chhe chhe inch chakkiyan
lines.literal: Utte nu main chhe chhe inch chakkiyan
lines.natural: I've put six‑inch wheels on them
lines.slangAware: I put six‑inch wheels on 'em
lines.chosen: On which I have placed six-inch wheels
lines.translated: On which I have placed six-inch wheels
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Utte nu main chhe chhe inch chakkiyan'. Glossary: conservative rendering; 'chakkiyan' uncertain (rims/pipes/parts).
lines.selectorReason: Followed glossary preferred phrasing to preserve the car-detail image while noting uncertainty about the exact part.

lines.original: Time laggu mitne nu
lines.literal: Time laggu mitne nu
lines.natural: It will take much time
lines.slangAware: It's gonna take a lot of time
lines.chosen: It will take much time
lines.translated: It will take much time
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Time laggu mitne nu'. Kept conservative: implies change/settling will need time.
lines.selectorReason: Chorus/refrain line — conservative, repeatable translation consistent with glossary guidance.

lines.original: Sanu thalle sittne nu
lines.literal: Sanu thalle sittne nu
lines.natural: To make us sit down
lines.slangAware: They try to put us down
lines.chosen: To make us sit down
lines.translated: To make us sit down
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Sanu thalle sittne nu'. Could mean 'to humble us' or 'to force us down'; literal phrasing retained for neutrality.
lines.selectorReason: Literal rendering preserves possible senses without overinterpreting context.

lines.original: Ikki vaari jor laa lai chhatti'an
lines.literal: Ikki vaari jor laa lai chhatti'an
lines.natural: Even if you try with full force twenty-one times
lines.slangAware: Even if you give it your all twenty-one times
lines.chosen: Even if you try 21 times with all your strength
lines.translated: Even if you try 21 times with all your strength
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Ikki vaari jor laa lai chhatti'an'. Glossary treats 'Ikki' as '21' here but notes uncertainty; rendered to match glossary preference.
lines.selectorReason: Followed glossary's preferred (conservative) '21 times' reading while flagging uncertainty.

lines.original: Fan ikko naar da main
lines.literal: Fan ikko naar da main
lines.natural: I am a fan of only one woman
lines.slangAware: I'm loyal—fan of just one girl
lines.chosen: I am a loyal admirer of only one woman
lines.translated: I am a loyal admirer of only one woman
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Fan ikko naar da main'. Glossary: 'fan' as English loanword and 'naar' = woman; kept literal and faithful.
lines.selectorReason: Clear statement of devotion; glossary gives preferred rendering.

lines.original: Das vaari vaarda main
lines.literal: Das vaari vaarda main
lines.natural: More than ten times
lines.slangAware: I do it over ten times
lines.chosen: More than ten times
lines.translated: More than ten times
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Das vaari vaarda main'. Numeric phrase; kept literal.
lines.selectorReason: Straightforward numeric/adverbial phrase—literal rendering is accurate.

lines.original: Ohde utton sau-sau diyan gatthiyan
lines.literal: Ohde utton sau-sau diyan gatthiyan
lines.natural: I shower her with 100‑rupee notes
lines.slangAware: I rain 100‑rupee notes on her
lines.chosen: I rain 100-rupee notes over her
lines.translated: I rain 100-rupee notes over her
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Ohde utton sau-sau diyan gatthiyan'. Glossary: conservative 'making it rain' image; rendered literally to avoid overstating tone.
lines.selectorReason: Preserves the slang image of showering money while staying conservative on tone.

lines.original: Time laggu mitne nu
lines.literal: Time laggu mitne nu
lines.natural: It will take much time
lines.slangAware: It's gonna take a lot of time
lines.chosen: It will take much time
lines.translated: It will take much time
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Time laggu mitne nu'. Kept conservative: implies change/settling will need time.
lines.selectorReason: Chorus/refrain line — conservative, repeatable translation consistent with glossary guidance.

lines.original: Sanu thalle sittne nu
lines.literal: Sanu thalle sittne nu
lines.natural: To make us sit down
lines.slangAware: They try to put us down
lines.chosen: To make us sit down
lines.translated: To make us sit down
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Sanu thalle sittne nu'. Could mean 'to humble us' or 'to force us down'; literal phrasing retained for neutrality.
lines.selectorReason: Literal rendering preserves possible senses without overinterpreting context.

lines.original: Ikki vaari jor laa lai chhatti
lines.literal: Ikki vaari jor laa lai chhatti
lines.natural: Even if you try 21 times with all your strength
lines.slangAware: Even if you come at me 21 times full force
lines.chosen: Even if you try 21 times with all your strength
lines.translated: Even if you try 21 times with all your strength
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Ikki vaari jor laa lai chhatti'. Matches earlier '21 times' variant; glossary uncertainty remains.
lines.selectorReason: Repeated motif — kept translation consistent with previous 'Ikki' lines per glossary guidance.

lines.original: Vall kaddta rekaane hunn tutte na crease
lines.literal: Vall kaddta rekaane hunn tutte na crease
lines.natural: I’ve styled myself up, girl — the crease won’t come undone now
lines.slangAware: I flexed my look, girl — the crease won’t fold no more
lines.chosen: I’ve styled myself up, girl, now the crease won’t break
lines.translated: I’ve styled myself up, girl, now the crease won’t break
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Vall kaddta rekaane hunn tutte na crease'. Metaphorical; 'crease' likely refers to a style/detail. Low confidence on exact image.
lines.selectorReason: Chose a conservative, literal metaphorical rendering that preserves the original image without overinterpreting; flagged low confidence.

lines.original: Thoda rukhe aa subaah de sanu aaundi ni tameez
lines.literal: Thoda rukhe aa subaah de sanu aaundi ni tameez
lines.natural: We're a bit rough in the mornings; manners aren't for us
lines.slangAware: Mornings make us rough — manners ain't our thing
lines.chosen: We're a bit rough in the mornings; we don't have manners
lines.translated: We're a bit rough in the mornings; we don't have manners
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Thoda rukhe aa subaah de sanu aaundi ni tameez'. 'Tameez' = manners; colloquial sense retained.
lines.selectorReason: Natural-sounding English that captures the colloquial self-description from the original.

lines.original: Aa lai tainu ditti saambh laa ni bebe wali chunni
lines.literal: Aa lai tainu ditti saambh laa ni bebe wali chunni
lines.natural: Here, I gave you this—keep that baby-girl's scarf safe
lines.slangAware: Here, I gave it to you—hold onto that baby girl's scarf
lines.chosen: Here, I gave you—keep that baby girl's scarf safe
lines.translated: Here, I gave you—keep that baby girl's scarf safe
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Aa lai tainu ditti saambh laa ni bebe wali chunni'. 'bebe' could be 'baby' or affectionate 'bebi' (beloved); kept literal 'baby girl' to avoid assuming nuance.
lines.selectorReason: Conservative rendering preserving the gift image and affectionate address while noting ambiguity in 'bebe'.

lines.original: Baitha double R vich paake bapu di kameez
lines.literal: Baitha double R vich paake bapu di kameez
lines.natural: Sitting in a Double R wearing my father's shirt
lines.slangAware: Chillin' in a Double R with dad's shirt on
lines.chosen: Sitting in a Double R wearing my father's shirt
lines.translated: Sitting in a Double R wearing my father's shirt
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Baitha double R vich paake bapu di kameez'. 'Double R' left literal (likely vehicle/brand); kept familial detail literal.
lines.selectorReason: Literal rendering preserves cultural/reference details without inventing specifics.

lines.original: Bahla door ni rekaane hun Ranjheyan nu kheda
lines.literal: Don't stay far — now I play with the Ranjhes
lines.natural: Don't stay far; I'll start messing with the Ranjhe lovers now
lines.slangAware: Don't go too far — I'll be playin' with the lovers now
lines.chosen: Don't stay far; I'll start playing with the Ranjhes now
lines.translated: Don't stay far; I'll start playing with the Ranjhes now
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Bahla door ni rekaane hun Ranjheyan nu kheda'. 'kheda' can mean tease/play or punish; 'Ranjhes' likely a group/name. Chose conservative 'playing' sense but confidence is low.
lines.selectorReason: Conservative choice to avoid aggressive reading; flagged low confidence due to lexical uncertainty.

lines.original: Bahla nede nu na jawan bas jhaaka ee bada mera
lines.literal: Don't go near; just glance — my pride is big
lines.natural: Don't come close; just look — my swagger's huge
lines.slangAware: Don't get close — just stare, my flex is massive
lines.chosen: Don't come close; just look — my swagger's huge
lines.translated: Don't come close; just look — my swagger's huge
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Bahla nede nu na jawan bas jhaaka ee bada mera'. 'jhaaka' rendered as glance/peep; 'bada' as big/pride/swagger.
lines.selectorReason: Natural, idiomatic English that captures boastful tone while staying faithful to the original's warning/showing-off line.

lines.original: Bahla ruka na rekaane kite maaran ikk fera
lines.literal: Don't be too arrogant, girl, or I'll spin back once more
lines.natural: Don't be too arrogant, girl, or I'll turn back and strike once more
lines.slangAware: Don't get too full of yourself, girl — I'll spin back one more time
lines.chosen: Don’t be too arrogant, girl, or I’ll spin back once more
lines.translated: Don’t be too arrogant, girl, or I’ll spin back once more
lines.note: Transliteration: Bahla ruka na rekaane kite maaran ikk fera. Glossary-prescribed conservative warning/retaliation reading retained.
lines.selectorReason: Matches glossary preferred rendering and fits surrounding warning/boast context; literal meaning preserved.

lines.original: Naddi do vall kha gayi ditta ikk vaari geda
lines.literal: The girl got all weak/flustered when I made just one round
lines.natural: The girl went weak when I made just one round
lines.slangAware: She went all weak when I circled just once
lines.chosen: The girl got all weak/flustered when I made just one round
lines.translated: The girl got all weak/flustered when I made just one round
lines.note: Transliteration: Naddi do vall kha gayi ditta ikk vaari geda. Kept literal 'one round' image per glossary.
lines.selectorReason: Directly follows glossary example; literal image maintained for accuracy.

lines.original: Kithe dabeya rekaane ehna dekh layi dabake
lines.literal: Where did I press to hold them? Girl — look closely and see the force of it
lines.natural: Where did I press to hold them? Girl — look closely and see the pressure
lines.slangAware: Where'd I press to lock 'em? Girl, look — feel the pressure I used
lines.chosen: Where did I press to hold them? Girl — look closely and see the force of it
lines.translated: Where did I press to hold them? Girl — look closely and see the force of it
lines.note: Transliteration: Kithe dabeya rekaane ehna dekh layi dabake. Ambiguity preserved as advised in glossary.
lines.ambiguity: High: could be literal pressing or a metaphorical 'pressure/force'.
lines.selectorReason: Retains intentional ambiguity between physical and metaphorical 'press/hold' while matching glossary wording.

lines.original: Karaan shopping'an Milan Afghan 'ali kha ke
lines.literal: I shop in Milan, enjoying Afghan treats
lines.natural: I shop in Milan and enjoy Afghan treats
lines.slangAware: I shop Milan-side, munchin' Afghan treats
lines.chosen: I shop in Milan, enjoying Afghan treats
lines.translated: I shop in Milan, enjoying Afghan treats
lines.note: Transliteration: Karaan shopping'an Milan Afghan 'ali kha ke. Place names left untranslated per glossary guidance.
lines.selectorReason: Follows glossary-prescribed rendering; simple, literal, and consistent with shopping motif.

lines.original: Tera darji rekaane change karde Mohali
lines.literal: Your tailor makes changes in Mohali
lines.natural: Your tailor does alterations in Mohali
lines.slangAware: Your tailor's doing tweaks over in Mohali
lines.chosen: Your tailor does alterations in Mohali
lines.translated: Your tailor does alterations in Mohali
lines.note: Transliteration: Tera darji rekaane change karde Mohali. Mohali kept as place name; tied to shopping/tailoring context.
lines.selectorReason: Natural-sounding English while staying close to literal meaning and surrounding clothing/shopping lines.

lines.original: Jutti pairan wich paavan tere Paris'on manga ke
lines.literal: I put shoes on my feet ordered from your Paris
lines.natural: I wear shoes I ordered from Paris for you
lines.slangAware: I rock shoes I had shipped from Paris, just for you
lines.chosen: I wear shoes I ordered from Paris for you
lines.translated: I wear shoes I ordered from Paris for you
lines.note: Transliteration: Jutti pairan wich paavan tere Paris'on manga ke. 'Tere' treated as address; Paris left untranslated.
lines.selectorReason: Conservative, clear rendering that preserves intent (luxury shoes ordered from Paris for the addressee).

lines.original: Tin mahine tin change ne location'an rekaane
lines.literal: Three months three changes are locations
lines.natural: Three months, three changes of location
lines.slangAware: Every three months I be switching locations three times
lines.chosen: Three months, three location changes
lines.translated: Three months, three location changes
lines.note: Transliteration: Tin mahine tin change ne location'an rekaane. Short, literal phrasing maintained to reflect ambiguity.
lines.ambiguity: Medium: could mean 'every three months I change location' or 'in three months there are three changes.'
lines.selectorReason: Keeps terseness and ambiguity of the original while rendering it idiomatically in English.

lines.original: Nitt UAE ton UK nu rotation'an rekaane
lines.literal: Daily UAE to UK rotations are kept
lines.natural: Every day I rotate between the UAE and the UK
lines.slangAware: I be rotating UAE↔UK nonstop
lines.chosen: Every day I rotate between the UAE and the UK
lines.translated: Every day I rotate between the UAE and the UK
lines.note: Transliteration: Nitt UAE ton UK nu rotation'an rekaane. Interpreted as frequent travel/rotation UAE↔UK.
lines.selectorReason: Clear, idiomatic rendering consistent with the travel/rotation theme in context.

lines.original: Main taan rukja dimag sala rukda ni mera
lines.literal: I indeed stop; the mind damn does not stop mine
lines.natural: I never stop; my mind doesn't rest
lines.slangAware: I don't stop for nothing; my head never rests
lines.chosen: I never stop; my mind doesn't rest
lines.translated: I never stop; my mind doesn't rest
lines.note: Transliteration: Main taan rukja dimag sala rukda ni mera. Tone smoothed into natural English while keeping emphasis.
lines.ambiguity: Medium: contains emphatic colloquial negation ('taan', 'sala') whose tone is approximated.
lines.selectorReason: Preserves original emphasis and meaning while smoothing colloquial particles into natural English.

lines.original: Tan hi laa laiye samadhi meditation'an rekaane
lines.literal: So we bring samadhi meditation sessions
lines.natural: So we take samadhi meditation sessions
lines.slangAware: So we hit deep samadhi meditation sessions
lines.chosen: So we take samadhi meditation sessions
lines.translated: So we take samadhi meditation sessions
lines.note: Transliteration: Tan hi laa laiye samadhi meditation'an rekaane. 'Samadhi' retained as specific meditation term per glossary.
lines.selectorReason: Conservatively keeps 'samadhi' untranslated and aligns with surrounding contrast between travel/work and rest.

lines.original: Loki sade kolon sikh hunn sanu den mattan
lines.literal: People now learn from us, they don't give us mattan
lines.natural: People now learn from us; they don't give us 'mattan'
lines.slangAware: Folks learn from us now; they ain't giving us no 'mattan'
lines.chosen: People now learn from us; they don't give us mattan
lines.translated: People now learn from us; they don't give us mattan
lines.note: Transliteration: Loki sade kolon sikh hunn sanu den mattan. 'Mattan' left as-is per glossary note to avoid guessing.
lines.ambiguity: High: meaning of 'mattan' is unclear (left untranslated).
lines.selectorReason: Maintains literal structure from source; flagged low confidence because 'mattan' is ambiguous and untranslated as instructed.

lines.original: Sanu kinna kuj de giyan ni mitti diyan vattan
lines.literal: These dirt paths have given us so much
lines.natural: These dirt paths have given us so much
lines.slangAware: These dusty roads gave us everything
lines.chosen: These dirt paths have given us so much
lines.translated: These dirt paths have given us so much
lines.note: Transliteration: Sanu kinna kuj de giyan ni mitti diyan vattan. Glossary-preferred literal rendering preserved for figurative flexibility.
lines.selectorReason: Directly matches glossary preferred rendering and preserves possible figurative meaning.

lines.original: Jithon langhe tera yaar maare camera flash
lines.literal: Where your guy passes, he strikes camera flash
lines.natural: Wherever your guy passes, the camera flashes
lines.slangAware: Wherever your boy walks, camera flashes pop
lines.chosen: Wherever your guy passes, the camera flashes
lines.translated: Wherever your guy passes, the camera flashes
lines.note: Transliteration: Jithon langhe tera yaar maare camera flash. Natural idiomatic English for paparazzi/camera-flash image.
lines.selectorReason: Clear, idiomatic and consistent with celebratory/celebrity image in the song.

lines.original: Hun khinch'de ne photo kade khinch de si lattan
lines.literal: Now they click photos; earlier they captured scars
lines.natural: Now they take photos; before, they recorded scars
lines.slangAware: Now it's all picture-time — back then they were taking the marks and scars
lines.chosen: Now they click photos, earlier they captured scars
lines.translated: Now they click photos, earlier they captured scars
lines.note: Transliteration: Hun khinch'de ne photo kade khinch de si lattan. Glossary advised conservative 'scars' rendering; uncertainty preserved.
lines.ambiguity: Medium: 'lattan' interpreted as 'scars' per glossary but term is uncertain.
lines.selectorReason: Follows glossary guidance; balances literalness with readability while flagging lexical uncertainty.

lines.original: Kehndi akh na lagge ve jad di mari tere utte
lines.literal: She says 'may the evil eye not fall' when she looked at you
lines.natural: She says, 'May the evil eye not touch you' when she looks at you
lines.slangAware: She goes, 'No jinx, baby,' whenever she stares at you
lines.chosen: She says, 'May the evil eye not touch you' when she looks at you
lines.translated: She says, 'May the evil eye not touch you' when she looks at you
lines.note: Transliteration: Kehndi akh na lagge ve jad di mari tere utte. Standard English idiom 'evil eye' used for clarity.
lines.ambiguity: Medium: 'jad di mari' is colloquial; interpreted as 'when she looks/looks at you.'
lines.selectorReason: Preserves cultural idiom ('akh na lagge') in an accessible English phrase while staying literal to context.

lines.original: Sanu sidha ne banage ni halaat sade puthe
lines.literal: They haven't made our conditions straight, our puthe
lines.natural: They haven't fixed our situation, man
lines.slangAware: They never fixed up our situation, man
lines.chosen: They haven't fixed our situation, man
lines.translated: They haven't fixed our situation, man
lines.note: Transliteration: Sanu sidha ne banage ni halaat sade puthe. 'Puthe' left out of translation due to uncertain meaning.
lines.ambiguity: High: function/meaning of 'puthe' unclear; omitted to avoid guessing.
lines.selectorReason: Conservative rendering that conveys core meaning while avoiding interpretation of a unclear slang term; low confidence noted.

lines.original: Tu taan hune ee tang aagyi biba nindran udake
lines.literal: You just now got bothered, girl, having taken away sleep
lines.natural: You just got upset, girl—you've lost sleep
lines.slangAware: You finally got fed up, babe—can't sleep 'cause of it
lines.chosen: You just now got upset, girl, having lost sleep
lines.translated: You just now got upset, girl, having lost sleep
lines.transliteration: ਤੂੰ ਤਾਂ ਹੁਣੇ ਹੀ ਤੰਗ ਆ ਗਈ ਬਿਬਾ ਨਿੰਦਰਾਂ ਉਡਾ ਕੇ
lines.note: Transliteration: Tu taan hune ee tang aagyi biba nindran udake. Preserves address 'biba' (girl) and sleep-loss image.
lines.ambiguity: Medium: 'tang aagyi' can mean irritated or exhausted; conservative reading used.
lines.selectorReason: Keeps conservative, literal reading aligned with immediate context about sleeplessness and worry.

lines.original: Sanu kai saal hogye naiyo changi tarah sutte
lines.literal: It has been many years; we have not slept properly
lines.natural: We haven't slept well in many years
lines.slangAware: We ain't had proper sleep in years, man
lines.chosen: We haven't slept well in many years
lines.translated: We haven't slept well in many years
lines.transliteration: ਸਾਨੂੰ ਕਈ ਸਾਲ ਹੋ ਗਏ ਨਹੀਂ ਚੰਗੀ ਤਰ੍ਹਾਂ ਸੁੱਤੇ
lines.note: Transliteration: Sanu kai saal hogye naiyo changi tarah sutte. Straightforward literal rendering.
lines.selectorReason: Clear literal meaning; idiomatic English phrasing chosen for naturalness and accuracy.

lines.original: Naara thaar ke te vairi rakhan saarh ke rekaane
lines.literal: Shouting slogans and keeping enemies tied up and returning them
lines.natural: Shouting slogans and keeping our enemies in check
lines.slangAware: We shout out and keep our rivals on a leash
lines.chosen: Shouting slogans and keeping our enemies in check
lines.translated: Shouting slogans and keeping our enemies in check
lines.transliteration: ਨਾਰਾ ਠਾੜ ਕੇ ਤੇ ਵੈਰੀ ਰੱਖਣ ਸਾਰ੍ਹ ਕੇ ਰੇਕਾਣੇ
lines.note: Transliteration: Naara thaar ke te vairi rakhan saarh ke rekaane. Conservative idiomatic rendering used; flagged uncertain.
lines.ambiguity: High: original phrase is idiomatic; exact action toward 'vairi' is unclear.
lines.selectorReason: Conservative interpretation of an idiomatic line that fits the song's boastful/defensive tone; low confidence due to ambiguity.

lines.original: Peende motran te chahan pattu kaarh ke rekaane
lines.literal: Sitting on motors and tearing/despoiling the desired cloth and returning
lines.natural: Riding the bikes and ripping away the cloth of desire
lines.slangAware: On the bikes, tearing off desire's fabric and moving on
lines.chosen: Riding the bikes and tearing away the cloth of desire
lines.translated: Riding the bikes and tearing away the cloth of desire
lines.transliteration: ਪੀੰਦੇ ਮੋਟਰਾਂ ਤੇ ਚਾਹਨ ਪੱਟੂ ਕੱੜ੍ਹ ਕੇ ਰੇਕਾਣੇ
lines.note: Transliteration: Peende motran te chahan pattu kaarh ke rekaane. 'Motran' treated as 'bikes'; 'chahan pattu' as metaphor for desire.
lines.ambiguity: High: several words (motran, chahan pattu) are slang/metaphor; preserved a conservative figurative reading.
lines.selectorReason: Maintains figurative image consistent with surrounding boastful lines while flagging lexical uncertainty.

lines.original: Tere bull'an de maare ne main vi ek M laata
lines.literal: Your lips hit me and I too made an M mark
lines.natural: Your kisses hit me, and I left my own mark
lines.slangAware: Your lips hit me—I left my own impression
lines.chosen: Your lips hit me, and I made an impression
lines.translated: Your lips hit me, and I made an impression
lines.transliteration: ਤੇਰੇ ਬੁੱਲਾਂ ਦੇ ਮਾਰੇ ਨੇ ਮੈਂ ਵੀ ਇੱਕ M ਲਾਟਾ
lines.note: Kept literal phrasing per glossary guidance to avoid over-interpreting slang 'M'.
lines.selectorReason: Matches glossary preferred literal rendering; conservative choice that preserves original slang ambiguity ('M') without inventing meaning.

lines.original: Haale navi aa Ferrari jutti jhaad ke rekaane
lines.literal: Just now there's a new Ferrari, having kicked off shoes and kept it
lines.natural: I just got a new Ferrari; I kick off my shoes and ride
lines.slangAware: Fresh Ferrari—kick off the juttis and roll
lines.chosen: I just got a new Ferrari; I kick off my shoes and ride
lines.translated: I just got a new Ferrari; I kick off my shoes and ride
lines.transliteration: ਹਾਲੇ ਨਵੀਂ ਆ ਫੇਰਾਰੀ ਜੁੱਤੀ ਝਾੜ ਕੇ ਰੇਕਾਣੇ
lines.note: Colloquial 'jutti jhaad ke' implies shaking off shoes before riding; preserved casual tone.
lines.ambiguity: Syntactic colloquialism makes exact nuance uncertain (literal removal vs. figurative).
lines.selectorReason: Preserves the casual image from the source while avoiding over-interpretation; literal action retained for lyrical naturalness.

lines.original: Dil todte main chhe chhe saak modte
lines.literal: I broke six hearts, turned six corners
lines.natural: I broke six hearts and took six turns
lines.slangAware: I broke six hearts, twisted six ways
lines.chosen: I broke six hearts, turned six corners
lines.translated: I broke six hearts, turned six corners
lines.transliteration: ਦਿਲ ਤੋੜਤੇ ਮੈਂ ਛੇ ਛੇ ਸਾਕ ਮੋੜਤੇ
lines.note: Phrase preserved per glossary to avoid misinterpreting local idiom.
lines.selectorReason: Directly follows glossary preferred rendering; avoids paraphrase that might change idiomatic meaning.

lines.original: Kataran wich atharan hor pakkian
lines.literal: There are eighteen more packed in the lines
lines.natural: There are eighteen more lined up
lines.slangAware: Got eighteen more lined up in the ranks
lines.chosen: There are eighteen more packed in the lines
lines.translated: There are eighteen more packed in the lines
lines.note: Numeric reading ('atharan' = eighteen) used literally; could be colloquial/count metaphor.
lines.ambiguity: Unclear if 'atharan' is literal '18' or a slang term; translated literally.
lines.selectorReason: Conservative, literal numeric rendering consistent with nearby numeric phrases and glossary guidance.

lines.original: Duron matha tekdiyan
lines.literal: From afar they bow their heads
lines.natural: They bow their heads from afar
lines.slangAware: They nod/bow way off in the distance
lines.chosen: They bow their heads from afar
lines.translated: They bow their heads from afar
lines.selectorReason: Straightforward literal translation that fits the context and flow of the surrounding lines.

lines.original: Mud mud dekh diyan
lines.literal: They look again and again / they keep turning to look
lines.natural: They keep looking back
lines.slangAware: They keep turning around to stare
lines.chosen: They keep looking back
lines.translated: They keep looking back
lines.selectorReason: Natural idiomatic English rendering that preserves the repetitive sense of the Punjabi line.

lines.original: Assi naiyo ikk vaari takkiyan
lines.literal: We have not waited even once
lines.natural: We haven't waited even once
lines.slangAware: We never waited a single time, bro
lines.chosen: We haven't waited even once
lines.translated: We haven't waited even once
lines.note: Verb 'takkiyan' could mean 'waited' or 'checked' in dialect; chosen conservative 'waited'.
lines.ambiguity: The verb's exact sense varies by dialect; conservative reading used.
lines.selectorReason: Conservative choice given dialectal uncertainty; keeps meaning safely aligned with context.

lines.original: Fan ikko naar da main
lines.literal: Fan ikko naar da main
lines.natural: I am a fan of only one woman
lines.slangAware: I'm loyal—fan of just one girl
lines.chosen: I am a loyal admirer of only one woman
lines.translated: I am a loyal admirer of only one woman
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Fan ikko naar da main'. Glossary: 'fan' as English loanword and 'naar' = woman; kept literal and faithful.
lines.selectorReason: Clear statement of devotion; glossary gives preferred rendering.

lines.original: Das vaari vaarda main
lines.literal: Das vaari vaarda main
lines.natural: More than ten times
lines.slangAware: I do it over ten times
lines.chosen: More than ten times
lines.translated: More than ten times
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Das vaari vaarda main'. Numeric phrase; kept literal.
lines.selectorReason: Straightforward numeric/adverbial phrase—literal rendering is accurate.

lines.original: Ohde utton sau sau diyan gatthiyan
lines.literal: Ohde utton sau-sau diyan gatthiyan
lines.natural: I shower her with 100‑rupee notes
lines.slangAware: I rain 100‑rupee notes on her
lines.chosen: I rain 100-rupee notes over her
lines.translated: I rain 100-rupee notes over her
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Ohde utton sau-sau diyan gatthiyan'. Glossary: conservative 'making it rain' image; rendered literally to avoid overstating tone.
lines.selectorReason: Preserves the slang image of showering money while staying conservative on tone.

lines.original: Time laggu mitne nu
lines.literal: Time laggu mitne nu
lines.natural: It will take much time
lines.slangAware: It's gonna take a lot of time
lines.chosen: It will take much time
lines.translated: It will take much time
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Time laggu mitne nu'. Kept conservative: implies change/settling will need time.
lines.selectorReason: Chorus/refrain line — conservative, repeatable translation consistent with glossary guidance.

lines.original: Sanu thalle sittne nu
lines.literal: Sanu thalle sittne nu
lines.natural: To make us sit down
lines.slangAware: They try to put us down
lines.chosen: To make us sit down
lines.translated: To make us sit down
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Sanu thalle sittne nu'. Could mean 'to humble us' or 'to force us down'; literal phrasing retained for neutrality.
lines.selectorReason: Literal rendering preserves possible senses without overinterpreting context.

lines.original: Ikki vaari jor laa lai chhatti'an
lines.literal: Ikki vaari jor laa lai chhatti'an
lines.natural: Even if you try with full force twenty-one times
lines.slangAware: Even if you give it your all twenty-one times
lines.chosen: Even if you try 21 times with all your strength
lines.translated: Even if you try 21 times with all your strength
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Ikki vaari jor laa lai chhatti'an'. Glossary treats 'Ikki' as '21' here but notes uncertainty; rendered to match glossary preference.
lines.selectorReason: Followed glossary's preferred (conservative) '21 times' reading while flagging uncertainty.

lines.original: Oh kudi jatt di fragrance door ton pehchane
lines.literal: That girl recognizes a jatt's fragrance from far away
lines.natural: That girl can smell a jatt's scent from afar
lines.slangAware: That girl can pick up a jatt's fragrance from way off
lines.chosen: That girl recognizes a jatt's fragrance from far away
lines.translated: That girl recognizes a jatt's fragrance from far away
lines.transliteration: ਉਹ ਕੁੜੀ ਜੱਟ ਦੀ ਫਰੇਗਰੈਂਸ ਦੂਰੋਂ ਪਛਾਣੇ
lines.selectorReason: Literal rendering that preserves cultural term 'jatt' and the sensory image; fits context directly.

lines.original: Asi aashqui ch nave ni shikari aa purane
lines.literal: We aren't new hunters in love, we're old ones
lines.natural: We're not new at love — we're seasoned hunters
lines.slangAware: We ain't rookie lovers — we're seasoned hunters
lines.chosen: We aren't new hunters in love, we're old ones
lines.translated: We aren't new hunters in love, we're old ones
lines.selectorReason: Literal, slightly stylized rendering that keeps the original contrast between 'new' and 'old' hunters in love.

lines.original: Gal sukkda kehndi tu jadon supne ch aavein
lines.literal: The talk dries up, you say, when you come in dreams
lines.natural: You say things die down when you come into my dreams
lines.slangAware: You say everything cools off when you appear in my dreams
lines.chosen: You say things die down when you come into my dreams
lines.translated: You say things die down when you come into my dreams
lines.note: 'Gal sukkda' literally 'the talk dries' — preserved conservatively as 'dies down'.
lines.ambiguity: Exact nuance of 'sukkda' (dry/die/calm) is fuzzy; chosen a neutral idiomatic option.
lines.selectorReason: Neutral idiomatic phrasing chosen to capture the likely poetic meaning without overstating any single nuance.

lines.original: Naddi ohdon di saundi aa pani rakh ke sirhane
lines.literal: The river's scent comes when water is kept by the pillow
lines.natural: The river's scent lingers when you keep water by the pillow
lines.slangAware: The river's smell sticks around when you put water by your pillow
lines.chosen: The river's scent lingers when you keep water by the pillow
lines.translated: The river's scent lingers when you keep water by the pillow
lines.note: Poetic image preserved literally rather than forcing a smoother paraphrase.
lines.ambiguity: Unclear whether this is literal or metaphoric; rendered literally.
lines.selectorReason: Kept the original poetic image intact; marked low confidence because the line may be figurative and its intended sense is uncertain.

lines.original: Main keha chalde rekaane sikke mitran de khote
lines.literal: I said 'walking', [about] coins — my friends' 'khote' (counterfeit/fake)
lines.natural: I said as they move, the coins are my friends' fakes
lines.slangAware: I said when they move, it's my friends' fake coins
lines.chosen: I said as they move, the coins are my friends' counterfeits
lines.translated: I said as they move, the coins are my friends' counterfeits
lines.note: Line is syntactically and idiomatically unclear; 'khote' usually means 'fake/counterfeit' — rendered conservatively.
lines.ambiguity: High uncertainty about exact grammar and referent.
lines.selectorReason: Conservative choice using the reliable meaning of 'khote' (fake/counterfeit); grammatical relationships in the line are ambiguous, so confidence is low.

lines.original: Aa ke behnde aa rekaane cheene mitran de kothe
lines.literal: They come and sit, having taken/robbed friends' kothe (houses/mansions)
lines.natural: They come and sit after taking friends' houses
lines.slangAware: They roll up and sit, after taking friends' houses
lines.chosen: They come and sit after taking friends' houses
lines.translated: They come and sit after taking friends' houses
lines.note: Could be literal theft or figurative takeover; kept literal wording.
lines.ambiguity: Unclear whether literal theft or figurative takeover; kept literal.
lines.selectorReason: Literal rendering chosen to avoid imposing a figurative interpretation; marked low confidence due to contextual uncertainty.

lines.original: Tainu sohniye rekaane karaan hathan naal chhavaan
lines.literal: To you, beautiful, I'll 'rekaane' do with my hands — touch
lines.natural: I'll touch you, beautiful, with my hands
lines.slangAware: Babe, I'll touch you with my hands
lines.chosen: I'll touch you, beautiful, with my hands
lines.translated: I'll touch you, beautiful, with my hands
lines.note: The repeated particle 'rekaane' is treated as a filler/vocative and omitted in English.
lines.ambiguity: 'rekaane' is treated as a filler/uncertain particle and omitted in English.
lines.selectorReason: Straightforward, conservative translation of a physical address; omitted uncertain filler to keep the line natural in English.

lines.original: Jivein karde ne chhavaan sadde pind'an de barote
lines.literal: Like they make explosions in our villages' gunpowder
lines.natural: Like they set off gunpowder in our village
lines.slangAware: Like they spark things off with our village gunpowder
lines.chosen: Like they set off gunpowder in our village
lines.translated: Like they set off gunpowder in our village
lines.note: Transliteration: Jivein karde ne chhavaan sadde pind'an de barote. Conservative, literal rendering of the explosive image to match surrounding 'gunpowder' references.
lines.ambiguity: Unclear whether 'chhavaan' refers to literal explosions, celebratory bangs, or figurative commotion.
lines.selectorReason: Keeps the gunpowder/explosion image consistent with nearby lines; conservative literal choice given ambiguity.

lines.original: Tera sau ton paar para hogyi dhilli kuj khaa ke
lines.literal: Your level/temperature went past one hundred after eating something from Delhi
lines.natural: Your level shot past a hundred after eating something from Delhi
lines.slangAware: You went past a hundred after snacking on Delhi stuff
lines.chosen: Your level/temperature went above 100 as you ate something from Delhi
lines.translated: Your level/temperature went above 100 as you ate something from Delhi
lines.note: Transliteration: Tera sau ton paar para hogyi dhilli kuj khaa ke. Followed glossary's conservative literal wording to avoid over-interpreting the metaphor.
lines.ambiguity: Unclear what 'sau' (hundred) precisely measures here (temperature, score, mood); 'dhilli kuj khaa ke' is vague about the Delhi reference.
lines.selectorReason: Uses glossary-preferred conservative rendering for ambiguous numeric/metaphorical reference.

lines.original: Teri 'taar da nazar gun powder chhuva ke
lines.literal: Your 'taar's gaze/spark touches gunpowder
lines.natural: Your string's spark brushes gunpowder
lines.slangAware: Your string's spark lights on gunpowder
lines.chosen: Your string’s spark touches gunpowder
lines.translated: Your string’s spark touches gunpowder
lines.note: Transliteration: Teri 'taar da nazar gun powder chhuva ke. Chosen per glossary guidance keeping 'taar' figurative and preserving the spark/gunpowder image.
lines.ambiguity: Unclear what 'taar' refers to (literal string, wire, instrument string, or metaphor); figurative image is uncertain.
lines.selectorReason: Glossary recommended a conservative figurative rendering; kept literal wording due to uncertain referent.

lines.original: Eh ton dar na rekaane dekh akhan wich paa ke
lines.literal: Don't be afraid of this, girl — look, put it in your eyes
lines.natural: Don't be afraid of this — look me in the eyes
lines.slangAware: Don't fear this, girl — look me straight in the eye
lines.chosen: Don't be afraid of this — look directly into my eyes
lines.translated: Don't be afraid of this — look directly into my eyes
lines.note: Transliteration: Eh ton dar na rekaane dekh akhan wich paa ke. Omitted the filler 'rekaane' in English and rendered as a direct vocative/imperative.
lines.ambiguity: The particle 'rekaane' appears repeatedly and is treated as filler; exact function is uncertain.
lines.selectorReason: Natural, conservative imperative that preserves original force while dropping repeated filler.

lines.original: Jadon chaldi Beretta dindi surma bana ke
lines.literal: When the Beretta moves/fires, it makes kohl (surma)
lines.natural: When the Beretta fires, it kicks up kohl-like dust
lines.slangAware: When the Beretta goes off it throws up kohl-like dust
lines.chosen: When the Beretta fires, it creates kohl-like dust
lines.translated: When the Beretta fires, it creates kohl-like dust
lines.note: Transliteration: Jadon chaldi Beretta dindi surma bana ke. Followed glossary's simile-preserving, literal approach despite low confidence.
lines.ambiguity: Unclear whether 'chaldi' modifies motion or sound; simile linking gun motion to kohl is figurative and syntactically uncertain.
lines.selectorReason: Keeps figurative image from glossary (gun motion → kohl) and matches surrounding violent/bright imagery.

lines.original: Kafi kar layi kamai kafi mitran ne udaalayi
lines.literal: Earned enough, friends have blown/spent a lot
lines.natural: I earned enough; my friends have spent a lot
lines.slangAware: Made enough cash — my boys have been splashing it around
lines.chosen: I earned enough; friends have spent a lot
lines.translated: I earned enough; friends have spent a lot
lines.note: Transliteration: Kafi kar layi kamai kafi mitran ne udaalayi. Conservative reading that preserves economic boast and friends' spending.
lines.ambiguity: 'udaalayi' likely means 'spent' or 'blew' (money); exact nuance is uncertain.
lines.selectorReason: Literal, conservative translation that keeps the money/spending sense without slang embellishment.

lines.original: Thode humble aa rekaane Lambo nivi karwali
lines.literal: You're a little humble, girl; I made the Lambo low
lines.natural: You're a little modest, girl; I had the Lambo lowered
lines.slangAware: A bit humble, babe — I dropped the Lambo's stance
lines.chosen: You're a little humble, girl; I had the Lambo lowered
lines.translated: You're a little humble, girl; I had the Lambo lowered
lines.note: Transliteration: Thode humble aa rekaane Lambo nivi karwali. Preserves automotive image (lowering suspension) and 'humble' remark.
lines.ambiguity: Unclear whether 'thode humble aa' addresses the singer or another person; context suggests addressing a girl.
lines.selectorReason: Maintains likely automotive meaning ('nivi karwali') and addresses the addressee as in context.

lines.original: Nave shoran ton rekaane gaddi chaldi ni sidhi
lines.literal: From new shocks/springs the car doesn't drive straight
lines.natural: With the new shocks, the car won't run straight
lines.slangAware: With fresh shocks the ride ain't running straight
lines.chosen: With the new shocks, the car doesn't run straight
lines.translated: With the new shocks, the car doesn't run straight
lines.note: Transliteration: Nave shoran ton rekaane gaddi chaldi ni sidhi. Chosen conservative automotive reading ('shocks') consistent with previous Lambo line.
lines.ambiguity: 'shoran' is uncertain—could mean shocks, springs, noises or another car part; impacts precise phrasing.
lines.selectorReason: Conservative technical interpretation chosen to keep car imagery coherent with adjacent lines.

lines.original: La daan meech ke ankhan nu back ganne di trolly
lines.literal: We squint our eyes and listen to sugarcane stalks crackle
lines.natural: We squint and listen to sugarcane stalks crackling
lines.slangAware: We squint our eyes and hear the sugarcane stalks crackle
lines.chosen: We squint our eyes and listen to sugarcane stalks crackle
lines.translated: We squint our eyes and listen to sugarcane stalks crackle
lines.note: Transliteration: La daan meech ke ankhan nu back ganne di trolly. Preserved the literal, unusual simile per glossary instruction.
lines.selectorReason: Glossary explicitly requested preserving this literal, unusual simile; high confidence.

lines.original: Lagge gabru di tod kudi tutti payi nu aave
lines.literal: It seems the young man's pull; the girl fell apart and came
lines.natural: The young man's pull seems to break the girl apart and bring her over
lines.slangAware: The guy's pull snaps her — the girl falls apart and comes running
lines.chosen: It seems the young man's pull; the girl fell apart and came
lines.translated: It seems the young man's pull; the girl fell apart and came
lines.note: Transliteration: Lagge gabru di tod kudi tutti payi nu aave. Conservative literal rendering chosen to avoid imposing a single interpretive reading.
lines.ambiguity: 'tutti payi nu aave' is idiomatic and could mean 'she fell for him' or 'she physically broke down and came'; precise sense is uncertain.
lines.selectorReason: Kept a literal/neutral translation because idiomatic meaning is uncertain in context.

lines.original: Age naar di fasal jiven phutti payi nu aave
lines.literal: Ahead, like the fire's crop, as if it got torn open and came
lines.natural: Ahead, like a field of fire that seems to have burst open
lines.slangAware: Ahead, like a fire-crop that's torn open and rushes out
lines.chosen: Ahead, like a fire's crop that seems to have burst open and come
lines.translated: Ahead, like a fire's crop that seems to have burst open and come
lines.note: Transliteration: Age naar di fasal jiven phutti payi nu aave. Preserved the metaphorical, image-rich phrasing conservatively.
lines.ambiguity: Imagery dense and idiomatic—'naar di fasal' and 'phutti payi nu aave' are metaphorical and open to interpretation.
lines.selectorReason: Literal, image-preserving choice to retain the original metaphor without over-interpretation.

lines.original: Meri yaad di maari nu tainu hichki rekaane
lines.literal: The blow/strike of my memory makes you have hiccups
lines.natural: My memory hits you and gives you hiccups
lines.slangAware: The hit of my memory makes you start hiccuping
lines.chosen: My memory hits you; it gives you hiccups
lines.translated: My memory hits you; it gives you hiccups
lines.note: Transliteration: Meri yaad di maari nu tainu hichki rekaane. Conservative figurative reading of 'memory hitting' resulting in a hiccup-like reaction.
lines.ambiguity: 'hichki' (hiccup) here is likely a figurative sudden reaction; exact connotation is unclear.
lines.selectorReason: Natural-sounding English that keeps the original metaphorical effect and matches adjacent lines.

lines.original: Sachi barish de gaane wangu sutti payi nu aave
lines.literal: Like the songs of real rain, she seems to have fallen asleep.
lines.natural: She seems asleep, like the songs of real rain.
lines.slangAware: She’s out like it’s a true rainy song.
lines.chosen: She seems asleep, like the songs of real rain.
lines.translated: She seems asleep, like the songs of real rain.
lines.note: Transliteration: Sachi barish de gaane wangu sutti payi nu aave. Conservative, slightly poetic rendering to preserve tone.
lines.ambiguity: The verb phrase 'sutti payi nu aave' is idiomatic — could mean 'seems asleep' or 'is soothed to sleep'; kept a conservative reading.
lines.selectorReason: Maintains soothing imagery and conservative interpretation per draft notes.

lines.original: Bechain hogyi ikk kudi fan hogyi
lines.literal: One girl became restless, became a fan.
lines.natural: One girl got restless and became a fan.
lines.slangAware: One girl got all restless — she turned into a fan.
lines.chosen: One girl got restless and became a fan.
lines.translated: One girl got restless and became a fan.
lines.note: Transliteration: Bechain hogyi ikk kudi fan hogyi. Straightforward, high-confidence translation.
lines.selectorReason: Direct, literal rendering matches plain meaning and fits context.

lines.original: Aujle de kanna wich nattiyan
lines.literal: In Aujla's ears there are 'nattiyan'.
lines.natural: She has nattiyan in her ears.
lines.slangAware: She's got nattiyan shining in her ears.
lines.chosen: With shiny earrings in her ears.
lines.translated: With shiny earrings in her ears.
lines.note: Transliteration: Aujle de kanna wich nattiyan. Rendered 'nattiyan' as 'shiny earrings' per glossary's conservative guidance.
lines.ambiguity: The exact referent ('Aujle' = Aujla) and who 'her' refers to is slightly unclear from line alone.
lines.selectorReason: Glossary advised preserving 'nattiyan' sense; chose a clear, conservative English phrasing.

lines.original: Kari di ni care kude gaul de ni sher kude
lines.literal: She doesn't care, dear; I am like a lion.
lines.natural: She doesn't care; I'm like a lion.
lines.slangAware: She ain't bothered, baby — I'm a lion.
lines.chosen: I don’t care much, dear, I’m like a lion.
lines.translated: I don’t care much, dear, I’m like a lion.
lines.note: Transliteration: Kari di ni care kude gaul de ni sher kude. Followed glossary-preferred conservative rendering contrasting her indifference with the speaker's bravado.
lines.selectorReason: Glossary gave this preferred conservative rendering; it fits the song's boastful tone.

lines.original: Bhedaan hon baara baari kathiyan
lines.literal: Even if twelve sheep gather together.
lines.natural: Even if twelve sheep gather together.
lines.slangAware: Even if a dozen sheep come together.
lines.chosen: Even if twelve sheep gather together.
lines.translated: Even if twelve sheep gather together.
lines.note: Transliteration: Bhedaan hon baara baari kathiyan. Kept literal phrasing (baara = twelve) as glossary recommended.
lines.ambiguity: Phrase is odd/literal; could be idiomatic in context but not clearly so here.
lines.selectorReason: Literal rendering preserves the original oddness; avoids inventing an idiomatic sense without evidence.

lines.translated: I rain 100-rupee notes over her
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Ohde utton sau-sau diyan gatthiyan'. Glossary: conservative 'making it rain' image; rendered literally to avoid overstating tone.
lines.selectorReason: Preserves the slang image of showering money while staying conservative on tone.

lines.original: (Time laggu mitne nu)
lines.literal: Time laggu mitne nu
lines.natural: It will take much time
lines.slangAware: It's gonna take a lot of time
lines.chosen: It will take much time
lines.translated: It will take much time
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Time laggu mitne nu'. Kept conservative: implies change/settling will need time.
lines.selectorReason: Chorus/refrain line — conservative, repeatable translation consistent with glossary guidance.

lines.original: (Sanu thalle sittne nu)
lines.literal: Sanu thalle sittne nu
lines.natural: To make us sit down
lines.slangAware: They try to put us down
lines.chosen: To make us sit down
lines.translated: To make us sit down
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Sanu thalle sittne nu'. Could mean 'to humble us' or 'to force us down'; literal phrasing retained for neutrality.
lines.selectorReason: Literal rendering preserves possible senses without overinterpreting context.

lines.original: (Ikki vaari jor laa lai chhatti'an)
lines.literal: Ikki vaari jor laa lai chhatti'an
lines.natural: Even if you try with full force twenty-one times
lines.slangAware: Even if you give it your all twenty-one times
lines.chosen: Even if you try 21 times with all your strength
lines.translated: Even if you try 21 times with all your strength
lines.note: Transliteration: 'Ikki vaari jor laa lai chhatti'an'. Glossary treats 'Ikki' as '21' here but notes uncertainty; rendered to match glossary preference.
lines.selectorReason: Followed glossary's preferred (conservative) '21 times' reading while flagging uncertainty.
